title: A copper PCB trace routing a state-transition signal from origin pad through a turn to an arrowhead destination. On a printed circuit board, a trace has no stray connections — only what the designer routed goes anywhere. Precept's transition declarations work exactly this way: declare the path and it exists; don't declare it and the signal has nowhere to go.
conceptualBridge: The PCB trace means Precept's event routing is a physically declared signal path because a circuit trace only connects what the designer declared — no undeclared route can be taken.
decision: Shortlisted after simplification: avg 4.38, no hard fails — v2 removes the grid leaving only the bold copper trace, origin pad, diagonal, arrowhead arms, and via ring; the via circle with punched center is the definitively PCB-specific element; copper on dark green is a unique color family in the set.
